preview_text:
The model favors Saudi Arabia at 41% to win, with Cape Verde trailing at 28%. The underdog's path to victory is notably narrower than the favorite's, though far from negligible in a match between evenly-matched regional sides.

The model's outcome spread reflects a moderately competitive fixture. A draw at 31% is the second-most-likely result, suggesting the teams are close enough in quality that neither escape cleanly. The 13-percentage-point gap between Saudi Arabia and Cape Verde indicates the favorite holds real structural advantage, yet the high draw probability tempers expectations of dominance.

Model and sportsbook consensus diverge meaningfully. The books assign Cape Verde an 8-point edge over the model (37% vs. 28%), while rating Saudi Arabia 6 points lower (35% vs. 41%). Polymarket prices align closely with the sportsbook consensus on both underdog and draw, but again favor Cape Verde relative to the model. The dashboard makes no claim as to which reading is correct; the variance suggests genuine uncertainty about the teams' relative strength on a neutral pitch.
